source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/04_G1717CW 외 7종/90_규격 Test report/1. 신청서 및 제출서류/07. Usability 문서/3. A-UEF-CYN_Usability Engineering File (Rev.0 250915).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering File for GT1717C and GT1717CP Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the usability engineering process, use specification, hazard-related use scenarios, and summative evaluation approach for the device.
keyPoints: The document specifies usability engineering for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
The stated purpose is to ensure safety by mitigating risks related to usability problems.
The document states compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015.
GT1717C and GT1717CP differ only in CsI deposition thickness and are identical in usability.
The intended user profile is Radiographer.
Hazard-related use scenarios include user manual review, preparation, product use, and maintenance.

concepts.name: Usability Engineering Process
concepts.evidence: The usability engineering process involves analyzing, designing, and verifying the user interface to ensure it meets usability and safety requirements.

concepts.name: Hazard-Related Use Scenario
concepts.evidence: use scenario that could lead to a hazardous situation or harm

concepts.name: Formative Evaluation
concepts.evidence: user interface evaluation conducted with the intent to explore user interface design strengths, weaknesses, and unanticipated use errors

concepts.name: Summative Evaluation
concepts.evidence: user interface evaluation conducted at the end of the user interface development with the intent to obtain objective evidence that the user interface can be used safely

concepts.name: Use Error
concepts.evidence: user action or lack of user action while using the medical device that leads to a different result than that intended by the manufacturer or expected by the user

concepts.name: Risk Control
concepts.pageTarget: Risk control for user interface design
concepts.evidence: To reduce use-related risk, the manufacturer shall use one or more of the following options, in the priority listed

concepts.name: User Interface of Unknown Provenance
concepts.evidence: user interface or part of a user interface of a medical device previously developed for which adequate records of the usability engineering process of this standard are not available

evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document. No. A-UEF-CYN Rev. No. 0

evidence.claim: The document applies to GT1717C and GT1717CP Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: This document specifies the usability engineering process for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Model: GT1717C, GT1717CP).

evidence.claim: Applicable standards and guidelines include IEC 60601-1-6, IEC 62366-1, IEC TR 62366-2, and EN ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-6 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6... IEC 62366-1 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering to medical Devices IEC TR 62366-2 Medical devices — Part 2... EN ISO 14971 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: GT1717C and GT1717CP differ only in CsI deposition thickness and are identical in usability.
evidence.evidenceText: The two products differ only in CsI deposition thickness, as shown below, and are identical in usability. - GT1717C : 300um - GT1717CP : 500um

evidence.claim: The intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital X-ray Imaging System is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic system.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended use

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended user profile is Radiographer.
The use environment includes hospital settings and mobile clinics.
evidence.evidenceText: Place Hospital settings and mobile clinics.

evidence.claim: The detector is a wired digital flat panel x-ray detector used with TFT glass and scintillator and supports automatic trigger signal detection technology.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a wired digital flat panel x-ray detector... The detector is used in combination with a TFT glass and scintillator and supports automatic trigger signal detection technology
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector operating principle

evidence.claim: Primary operating functions include reading the user manual, connecting components, calibration, power on/off, checking indicators, transportation, positioning the patient imaging area, and cleaning verification.
evidence.evidenceText: Read the user manual... Connect product components Product calibration Prodcut power ON/OFF Check the indicator Product transportation. Place the patient's imaging area on the detector Cleaning method and verification of cleaning results

evidence.claim: Known hazards and hazardous situations associated with use errors were not identified from similar medical device user interfaces.
evidence.evidenceText: However, there were no hazards and hazardous situations associated with use errors.

evidence.claim: Foreseeable use errors include not reading instructions thoroughly, not understanding safety precautions, unfamiliarity with connection, calibration, power operation, indicator status, dropping the product, inability to identify the shooting area, and inappropriate cleaning.
evidence.evidenceText: Not reading the instructions thoroughly... The user does not understand the precautions... The user is not familiar with how to connect... calibration method... turn the product on and off... indicator status... Dropping the product... not able to identify the shooting area... User cleans in an inappropriate manner.

evidence.claim: All identified usage scenarios were used for summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All usage scenarios we identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: The summative evaluation report is recorded in A-UVR-CYN.
evidence.evidenceText: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-UVR-CYN).

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text, although source metadata includes authoritySignal CE/MDR.
Revision metadata is conflicting: the cover and header state Rev. No. 0, while the accompanying document table lists A-UEF-CYN Rev. 1 for Usability Engineering File.
Some text contains OCR or source quality issues, including misspellings such as 'Prodcut' and a reference to section 5.4 when the visible section is 4.4.
